item.content:encoded:
Nice!

I really wanna know more about that mysterious force

item.title: Staff Post - Open
item.content:encoded: The image that was used in this article is non-compliant with the SCP wiki's license (CC BY-SA 3.0) as, across multiple sources, there has been no indication it has been released under Creative Commons. Thus, the image has been removed.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
What if they put some other similar non anomalous statues in containment with it? Maybe it would change to other forms if given some ideas/hosts.

Especially with whatever is chasing it having found it. Might be looking for a new form to hide again.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Hey look, it's Rusty!

I love how the mascot of the Iowa Museum of Natural History is now an SCP.

+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Like, two-thousand years ago, the animating force dated this super-predator, and it turns out she's frickin' crazy. So he turned into a sloth, and now she found him.

Run, bro.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Woah, that poor extradimensional animating force is being hunted by something we're going to have a damn hard time protecting it against. And that something needs to be captured and provided an SCP designation. And forced to stop attacking stuff. Or whatever.

Seriously this thing is what and I love it. +1.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Okay, I really like this. The simple fact that it's a giant sloth makes me smile, but the notes in particular set it apart.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I didn't have high hopes after glancing at the picture, but wow, you really pulled it together at the end. The implied backstory was a nice touch.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I like it even more now after the edits. Good job.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: The edits have secured my upvote.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Trying something new to get rid of Agent McComb's note, which I was not a fan of myself. I think it's an improvement, but I'll be interested to see what you guys think of the new Incident Log.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: *twitch* Loathe.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I like the first note, but the second note really ruins it. It's just awfully forced. It's speculation on top of speculation, and it exists for no reason other than to try to add a veneer of creepiness to an article that honestly doesn't need it.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: as you may or may not be able to tell I love prehistoric mammals. so yeah, upvote

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
While you did flesh out this idea rather well it just kept screaming Night at the Museum.

Screw that, it was pretty good. The notes were the icing on the cake. +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Congrats kid, you got yourself a Bright upvote. Don't go spending it all in one place.

I was iffy until the notes. They really bring a whole new view on the item.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Oops!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Extant means surviving.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
In its inactive (or camouflage) state, SCP-1405 appears to be a normal model: X-ray scans indicate it is composed primarily of plastic resin on an internal metal scaffolding.

Hmmm, couldn't they put in a modified and disguised X-ray scanner in the enclosure, and lure it into walking through it?

but a very small number (<1%) are mammalian hairs with DNA similar to that of extant sloth lineages.

How do they have the DNA of extinct sloths for comparison? Shouldn't they be comparing it to the DNA of living sloths?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Fair 'nuff. I shall think about how to streamline this…

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: The problem is , it isn't relevant because it isn't supported by evidence, and it doesn't even imply any action we have to take.It's just some researcher musing idly.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: It's interesting how polarizing those notes are. Would you care to elaborate on why you don't like McComb's? Seems like he's getting a love it or loath it response.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Sheesh, you are right. Quotations purged.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: To be honest, I agree with you and VAElynx…I'm not a huge fan of the notes. My dilemma was that, since that entire hypothesis isn't really a scientific one, it doesn't belong in the containment protocols or description, but it is still relevant to containing the critter. It doesn't make for very elegant storytelling though. I think I need to come up with some more observable characteristics that would justify the "something else animating the sloth" idea.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I love this as a giant sloth with an anomalous protective adaption which has somehow allowed it to survive for hundreds of thousands of years. Even though the article points to a recent animation.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Not bad at all, very cute, until freaking McCombs comes in and ruins it at the end. That said, looking at the other comments, I don't think you'd lose anything by removing the pair of notes. Just in case you consider it, of course.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Aww, he looks so cuddly. I realy love this one for many reasons and I congratulate you on the work you did since the draft.

I found the description a bit long, but it reads very well. I agree somewhat with Scantron's comment: it would be even better if you could imply it more subtly, but I think it's still very good like that.

Minor comments:

"treats": I don't think it need to be in quotation marks.

Megalonyx should probably be in italics.

I don't think it's very useful to blank the last digits of the date (I won't spend sleepless nights wondering if it's 1992 or 1998).

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Eeek, that last note wasn't there when I read it in the forums. It adds so much. Have an upvote good sir.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I think you should use the "quotes" less, but other than that, aaah, this type of skip it right up my alley <3 Happily +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: See, I like the idea of what you're trying to get at, but you need to be less sledgehammer-y with it at the end and make it more apparent in the article itself.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Thanks for that, I saw something went funky when I was posting it.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
It's not a theory, it's a hypothesis.
It also feels far too baseless speculation to have a place in the article.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I like it.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Kenji_and_co, I fixed the page name and image subtitle.

Otherwise, I like this, although I'm not quite sure why. +1
